summary: Kenneth demonstrates a $1,900 weekly return using covered calls across multiple stocks (HIMS, D-Wave, SoundHound, Marin, APLD), emphasizing that the strategy's goal is capturing some premium rather than maximizing directional gains. He illustrates disciplined position management, including handling assignment and redeploying capital.
takeaways: The covered call goal is to make some of the money, not all of it—accept partial profits rather than chase full upside
Minimum capital requirement is $10,000 to buy 100 shares of a $100 stock to sell 1 covered call contract
Weekly covered call returns of $454–$631 per position are achievable on $17,000–$40,000 deployments (roughly 1.1%–4.3% per week)
When shares are assigned, immediately redeploy capital to new covered call positions the following trading day
Even significant unexpected upside (MARIN from $40 to $50) can coexist with successful covered call execution if strike is set below the spike
Do not try to predict or time explosive stock moves; focus on consistent premium collection across multiple positions
Holding losing positions (QuantumCape from $11.40 to $7) and selling covered calls against them can recover losses when stock rebounds

strategy_candidates.change: Implement a minimum premium capture target of 3–4% per week across the portfolio by selecting strike prices and expirations that balance assignment risk and consistent income
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth achieved 4.3% on the full $43,478 portfolio in one week; his per-position returns ranged from 1.1% to 2.7%, suggesting a 3–4% portfolio-level target is realistic and aligns with the Premium Desk goal of $1,250/month (~2.5% on $50k)

title: Part 17: 9/22-9/26 -  I made $1900 in ONE WEEK Trading Covered Calls. Here's How.
